author: Dr. Priya Nair (Transplant Hepatology)
content:
MULTIDISCIPLINARY LIVER TRANSPLANT EVALUATION — UNIVERSITY TRANSPLANT CENTER
Date: 5/20/2027

SUMMARY: 50F with decompensated alcohol-related cirrhosis (recurrent diuretic-refractory ascites, right hepatic hydrothorax, hepatic encephalopathy). MELD-Na in the high-20s. Recent ICU admission for flash pulmonary edema attributed to peri-procedural FFP overload during outpatient drainage — since corrected.

CANDIDACY: Sustained alcohol abstinence ~14 months with documented engagement in an outpatient recovery program and AA; negative screens. Strong social support (sister). Vaccinations complete (hep A/B, pneumococcal). Cardiac and other pre-transplant workup acceptable. No HCC.

DECISION: Listed for deceased-donor liver transplant. Her recurrent fluid burden and the morbidity it causes (including the recent transfusion-related ICU stay) underscore the need for definitive therapy via transplant. Bridge with the least-harmful drainage strategy in the interim (no further prophylactic FFP).

PLAN: Active on the list; continue recovery program; hepatology to manage interim symptoms; PleurX in place for hydrothorax control.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Priya Nair, MD (Transplant Hepatology). 5/20/2027.
